candidates.title: fix #488 - support user field for completion
candidates.body:
- a few other backends use `user` instead of `username` or `login`
  (e.g. gopass-jsonapi, keepass2csv importer, fpm2 importer)
- shorter to type when extracting field separately (e.g. CLI/clipboard) tools

fixes #488
issue_titles: Add support for user: as well as username: for gopass repos
issue_bodies:
passforios looks for the string "username" when displaying login names and filling in forms in iOS.

The gopass-jsonapi writes "user" when writing to gopass

https://github.com/gopasspw/gopass-jsonapi/blob/main/internal/jsonapi/responses.go#L196

Can we get passforios to look for the strings "username", "login" and "user" to cover more backends?
candidates.author: MartinNowak

candidates.title: Add handoff support for code blob views
candidates.body:
I'm using handoff quite extensively (it's a great feature for me). Thus I did code blob view handoff just because sometimes it's easier to read code in a bigger screen.

There were no feature requests and from #331 I see that it's not expected that people will use handoff much. So if it looks like not a useful feature feel free to close it.
issue_bodies:
Ref #328.

While implementing this I was wondering though... opening a web page with Handoff would automatically mark an issue as read (as you would load the page on GitHub.com). Do we maybe want to only allow Handoff when the user has this setting enabled?

This would only make sense for the issues though - we can still support handoff for readmes, profiles etc. Not sure what would make most sense here.

I noticed that my inbox doesn’t have the expected 🎅 icon today. It looks like the code that sees if there’s a special icon looks first for a mapping for this year, then for a fixed mapping. Instead, it should try to get the **day** from each key. Example code: ```swift let today = data["\(year)"]?["\(month)"]?["\(day)"] ?? data["fixed"]?["\(month)"]?["\(day)"] ``` Merry Christmas!

candidates.title: MBL-2970: Clean up interface and mock interface for fetching manage pledge backings
candidates.body:
<!-- This template is **just a guide**, delete any and all parts which you don't need! -->

# 📲 What

1. Rename `fetchBackingWithIncrementsRefundedAmount` to `fetchBackingForManagePledge`
2. Split the MockService stored results for `fetchBackingForManagePledge` and `fetchBacking`, so they can return different data
3. Update Project tests that used mock data for `fetchBackingForManagePledge` to use `fetchBacking` results
4. Change type of `fetchBackingForManagePledge` from `SignalProducer<ProjectAndBackingEnvelope>` to `SignalProducer<Backing>`

# 🤔 Why

This untangling will let me delete a bunch of redundant GraphQL code. Currently we are fetching an extra `Project` (and `...ProjectFragment`) when we fetch the backing for the Manage Pledge page.

By changing these types and cleaning all this up, updating the actual query will be very simple.

candidates.title: Bugfix FXIOS-14809 [Translations] improve performance by wrapping AS methods
candidates.body: ## :scroll: Tickets [Jira ticket](https://mozilla-hub.atlassian.net/browse/FXIOS-14809) [Github issue](https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/firefox-ios/issues/31450) ## :bulb: Description We initially had other hangs that were addressed in this [PR](https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/firefox-ios/pull/31605). They were related to a specific `getAttachments` method, but now we are seeing other hangs for `getRecords`, although they are occurring at a lesser scale (~8% of hangs in 147.2.1). Similarly to the other case, the issue is that getRecords was blocking main thread and the solution in this PR is to wrap the call in an async method. **Hangs in Main** **No hangs in PR** Note: **Copied over from other PR** The main issue is with A/S having these Rust methods on main thread vs background thread when they make network calls that can hog up the main thread. Apparently this issue isn't isolated to the translation and there are other methods that should be async as well (seems this was discussed with Relay work too). This work will be capture in this follow up and would require collaboration with A/S team [ticket](https://mozilla-hub.atlassian.net/browse/FXIOS-14616). ## :pencil: Checklist - [x] I filled in the ticket numbers and a description of my work - [x] I updated the PR name to follow our [PR naming guidelines](https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/firefox-ios/wiki/Pull-Request-Naming-Guide) - [x] I ensured unit tests pass and wrote tests for new code - [ ] If working on UI, I checked and implemented accessibility (Dynamic Text and VoiceOver) - [ ] If adding telemetry, I read the [data stewardship requirements](https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/firefox-ios/wiki/Adding-Glean-Telemetry-Events) and will request a data review - [ ] If adding or modifying strings, I read the [guidelines](https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/firefox-ios/wiki/How-to-add-and-modify-Strings) and will request a string review from l10n - [ ] If needed, I updated documentation and added comments to complex code

candidates.title: Added ScreenshotTestHelpers
candidates.body:
# 📲 What

- Add unified screenshot test helpers (ScreenshotType, forEachScreenshotType, forEachScreenshotType(withData:), assertAllSnapshots, assertSnapshot overloads for UIViewController/UIView/SwiftUI).
- Centralize 4- and 5-way orthogonalCombos in the shared combos helper.
- Standardize snapshot naming and environment/trait setup; shorten snapshot names.

# 🤔 Why
- Snapshot tests were inconsistent (device/language sets, dark mode/font handling, manual names).
- Reduce churn and ensure predictable coverage across devices, languages, light/dark, and Dynamic Type while keeping counts low.
# 🛠 How

- forEachScreenshotType: orthogonal coverage of all devices/languages/light/dark/regular+large fonts (caps total to max list size).
- forEachScreenshotType(withData:): adds an extra data dimension (e.g., intents) without exploding combinations.
- assertAllSnapshots: owns the loop when you just need a controller factory and types.
- assertSnapshot overloads:

-UIViewController
-UIView (optional intrinsic sizing)
-SwiftUI View (optional intrinsic sizing)

- Snapshot names now: File_TestFunc_<device>_<language> (shortened).
- Updated LoginToutViewControllerTests to use the helpers as a concrete example.
- Switched to the non-deprecated SnapshotTesting API (of:).

candidates.title: Use a smaller fragment when you fetch the backer attached to a Backing
candidates.body:
# 📲 What

This uses `PublicUserFragment` when you fetch the `backer` associated with a `Backing`.

(This review follows review #2748)

# 🤔 Why

1. I didn't run any exacting profiling, but cleaning this up leads to a perceivable improvement in the performance of the Profile page on my account. We were recursively fetching your Backings, effectively by querying `Backing { backer { backings } }` when `UserFragment` calling `backings`.
2. `UserFragment` queries a lot of private fields. While in many cases, this is  correct - because you're usually looking at your own backings - it's not always true. If you're a creator messaging a backer, we use `BackingFragment` to fetch the _backer's_ backing, and then you should only be able to see public fields.


# 🤨  Why now?

I'm working on some refactoring to the Project page, focusing on improving its speed (both perceived, and actual). This GraphQL cleanup is an important simplification step as I dig into the guts of how the project page loads.

Creating this more limited fragment makes the code clearer, and eliminates a bunch of unnecessary variable passing in GraphQL.
issue_titles: Use a smaller fragment when you fetch the creator attached to a Project
issue_bodies:
# 📲 What

This creates a new fragment, `PublicUserFragment`, which is used by `ProjectFragment` to fetch the project's creator.

# 🤔 Why

`ProjectFragment` was always fetching a full `UserFragment`, but `UserFragment` is really designed to be used when fetching the _current_ user. It includes a lot of private information, like newsletter subscriptions, the user's stored cards, and so on. None of this was relevant if you're just grabbing information about who made a project, unless it happened to be yours. The backend was returning `null` for all of those fields.

Creating this more limited fragment makes the code clearer, and eliminates a bunch of unnecessary variable passing in GraphQL.

# 🤨  Why now?

I'm working on some refactoring to the Project page, focusing on improving its speed (both perceived, and actual). This GraphQL cleanup is an important simplification step as I dig into the guts of how the project page loads.

candidates.title: Convert Xcode frameworks to Swift packages
candidates.body:
This PR is a caffeine fueled nightmare. I am so sorry but also you're welcome?

# 📲 What

This pull request converts our three existing Xcode frameworks (KsApi, Library, and Kickstarter-Framework) to be Swift packages. It removes the old targets and integrates the packages into the Xcode project. And it updates the CI so that all packages are built and tested.

# 🤔 Why

Legacy Xcode frameworks and Swift packages seem to have trouble reliably linking and deduplicating code. For example, we use the Apollo library for GraphQL. If we include this in an Xcode framework like Library, and then in a Swift package like ServerDrivenUI that then gets included into that same Xcode framework, that will include two copies of the Apollo symbols in the final binary. When Swift does existential checks `if let operation = op as? GraphQLOperation { … }`, these symbols might not be called the same, and the check will fail or the app will crash.

This is a blocker for moving forward on ServerDrivenUI and our other options failed. And there are hundreds of duplicated symbol warnings which are all bugs lying in waiting.

# 🛠 How

1. Basic packages were created for KsApi, Library, and Kickstarter-Framework.
2. A tool was vibe-coded to use the [XcodeProj](https://github.com/tuist/XcodeProj) package to read through all of the files in the targets and `git mv` each individual file into the appropriate package folder. (A model did not do the actual moving of files or interact with file content at all.)
3. Tests and test support files were split up into new targets like `KsApiTestHelpers` and `LibraryTestHelpers`
4. Hundreds of test files were manually updated to use these new targets and helpers.
5. Each package got its own xcassets copy and Bundles were updated in various files. Also manually. Because I love suffering.
6. Once tests were passing individually, the bundles were integrated into the iOS app and the old targets were completely removed.
7. Since some tests for Library rely on a test host, they were restored in an Xcode test bundle, rather than a Swift one. Tests shouldn't be added to this unless they crash otherwise.
8. CI scripts were updated so that each package has its own test runner.

Genuinely I cannot believe this worked.

# ✅ Acceptance criteria

God how do you even review this. We decided that we would just all look through the project and smoke test everything.

# ⏰ TODO

- [x] Deduplicate xcassets
- [x] Fix Danger yelling at me (or disable it for this PR and restore it later)
- [x] Fix Fastlane distribution builds
candidates.author: stevestreza-ksr

candidates.title: Use a smaller fragment when you fetch the creator attached to a Project
candidates.body:
# 📲 What

This creates a new fragment, `PublicUserFragment`, which is used by `ProjectFragment` to fetch the project's creator.

# 🤔 Why

`ProjectFragment` was always fetching a full `UserFragment`, but `UserFragment` is really designed to be used when fetching the _current_ user. It includes a lot of private information, like newsletter subscriptions, the user's stored cards, and so on. None of this was relevant if you're just grabbing information about who made a project, unless it happened to be yours. The backend was returning `null` for all of those fields.

Creating this more limited fragment makes the code clearer, and eliminates a bunch of unnecessary variable passing in GraphQL.

# 🤨  Why now?

I'm working on some refactoring to the Project page, focusing on improving its speed (both perceived, and actual). This GraphQL cleanup is an important simplification step as I dig into the guts of how the project page loads.

candidates.title: big refactoring: UnifiedKey/を導入し、FlickとQwertyの実装を共通化
candidates.body:
resolve #553 
## 全体概要
このプルリクエストでは、キーボードテーマとキーのモデルに関するロジックを整理し、レイアウト依存の特殊処理を排除してコードを単純化しています。テーマアクセスを静的プロパティ化することで扱いやすくし、保守性を向上させています。

---

## 主な変更点

### テーマ処理の単純化
- QWERTYやフリックといったレイアウトに依存した色分けやハイライト処理を削除。
- `AzooKeySpecificTheme.native` と `default` を関数から静的プロパティに変更し、コード全体で利用箇所を修正。

### レイアウト依存ロジックの削除
- `Design.colors` のカラー取得処理をレイアウト非依存のプロパティに変更。
- フォント選択ロジックを修正し、レイアウト依存を削除。代わりにテキストの長さに基づいてフォントサイズを決定。

### プロトコルとモデルの一般化
- `ApplicationSpecificKeyboardViewExtensionLayoutDependentDefaultThemeProvidable` を静的プロパティベースに変更。
- `QwertyCustomKeysValue.compiled` がより汎用的な `[any UnifiedKeyModelProtocol<Extension>]` を返すように変更。
- `KeyLabelType` に `Sendable` と `Equatable` を実装し、並行処理の安全性と比較の容易さを向上。

### クリーンアップと小改善
- `LongpressActionType.none` を public に公開。
- `VariableStates` のドキュメントを改善し、バリエーション空間変化の検出に使うことを明示。

### 不要コードの削除
- 旧 `FlickKeyModelProtocol.swift` を削除し、フリックキー専用の背景スタイル処理を廃止。

---

## 効果
- テーマとキー描画の処理がレイアウトに依存しなくなり、コードの一貫性と保守性が向上。
- プロトコル・モデルの一般化により再利用性が高まり、並行処理でも安全に利用可能に。

candidates.title: NineAnimatorCore - System for Building JavaScript Sources
candidates.body:
NineAnimatorCore is a framework for building NineAnimator parsers using JavaScript. This PR is part of our move from a completely native system to a hybrid architecture, which will allow for easier maintenance and distribution of the app. Below are some benefits of this new architecture.

* Development - NACore bundles will be using JavaScript for source and server parsers, therefore allowing more developers to contribute to the project;
* Distribution - NACore allows NineAnimator to update the parsers by downloading a new bundle, avoiding the need for making new releases.

**NineAnimatorCore will be worked on in a separate repository from now on, as part of our effort to modularize the app.**

## Core Components

- **NineAnimatorCore Engine** (`NACoreEngine`): engine for running the JavaScript environment
  - [x] Basic JS virtual machine and contexts management & maintenance
  - [x] Basic type conversions (most are handled by `JSValue`)
  - [ ] Custom types & conversions
    - [x] `NineAnimatorPromise` and JavaScript `Promise`
    - [x] `AnimeLink`, `EpisodeLink`
    - [x] `Anime`, `Episode`
    - [x] `PlaybackMedia`
    - [ ] `NineAnimatorError` custom error types
    - [ ] `ContentProvider` interface for paged lists of links
    - [ ] `TrackingContext`?
  - [x] Network requests through `NineAnimator.fetch` (WIP)
  - [ ] Logging APIs
- **NineAnimatorCore BaseSource** (`NACoreBaseSource`): implements the `Source`/`PromiseSource` protocol. Bridging to existing source systems.
  - [ ] JavaScript `BaseSource` specifications
  - [ ] Source method bridges
- **NineAnimatorCore VideoProviderParser** (`NACoreMediaProviderParser`): implements the `VideoProviderParser` protocol. Bridging to existing server parser systems.
  - [ ] JavaScript `VideoProviderParser` specifications
  - [ ] Parser method bridges
  - [ ] Parser registry bridges
- **NineAnimatorCore Bundles**: zip container structures for code/assets delivery
  - [ ] Bundle structure specification (for code and resources)
  - [ ] Manifest format specification
  - [ ] Singature verification
  - [ ] `NineAnimatorCloud` integration
  - [ ] `NACoreBundleLoader` bundle loading implementation
